_comment: Milestone 6 (REBUILD_PLAN.md) -- 8 fixtures, expected values recorded by manually reading each PDF's real text (see the session's fixture-selection notes in MILESTONE_LOG.md for exactly how). 7 are real resumes from data/real_resumes/ (the actual hackathon dataset); 1 (mock_cgpa_percentage) is synthetic, explicitly marked 'source': 'synthetic' below, not passed off as real -- included because PROJECT_CONTEXT.md Section 11 confirms no percentage-format CGPA exists anywhere in the real 54-resume dataset, so a percentage-format case can't be sourced from real data at all.
_expected_skills_note: expected_skills is a reasonable, non-exhaustive subset of what's actually listed on each resume (a judgment call, per REBUILD_PLAN.md's Milestone 6 scope) -- the clearest, least-ambiguous skill tokens most likely to be correctly extracted by a competent reader, not every granular item on the page. Skill recall is computed case-insensitively with substring containment in either direction (e.g. 'React' matches an extracted 'React.js'), since exact string equality would unfairly fail on reasonable rephrasing that isn't actually a miss.

fixtures.notes: No CGPA/GPA/percentage anywhere on this resume (education line is just 'B.Tech, Computer Science -- IIIT Hyderabad, 2016-2020') -- expected_cgpa_10pt is null on purpose, this fixture is here for name/skill accuracy, not CGPA accuracy.
No CGPA/GPA/percentage on this resume either -- same reasoning as above.

PyMuPDF block-position check confirms this triggers the genuine two-column split path in src/extraction/pdf_reader.py::_column_aware_text (verified locally this session, not assumed). CGPA stated plainly as 'CGPA: 8.4 / 10.0'.
Confirmed two-column via the same local block-position check. CGPA stated plainly as 'CGPA: 9.0 / 10.0'.
Confirmed two-column via the same local block-position check. CGPA stated plainly as 'CGPA: 8.7 / 10.0'.
No CGPA/GPA/percentage present (education line is just 'B.Tech, Computer Science -- COEP Pune, 2016-2020').
No CGPA/GPA/percentage present (M.Sc/B.Sc lines have no grade at all).

SYNTHETIC, not a real hackathon-dataset resume -- included specifically because PROJECT_CONTEXT.md Section 11 confirms zero percentage-format CGPA strings exist anywhere in the real 54-resume dataset. Resume states 'Percentage: 82%'; PROJECT_CONTEXT.md Section 7's formula is cgpa = value / 9.5 = 82 / 9.5 = 8.6315..., rounds to 8.63 -- matches the already-documented synthetic verification in PROJECT_CONTEXT.md Section 11.5 ('82% -> 8.63, percentage').
